articles.title: TURN YOUR CHATGPT BRAINSTORMS INTO FIGJAM DIAGRAMS
articles.summary: ChatGPT users can now generate FigJam diagrams—including flowcharts, gantt charts, state diagrams, and sequence diagrams—directly from conversations, documents, and photos. The feature transforms these inputs into collaborative starting points within FigJam. It is currently available for Free, Pro, and Plus plan users, with Enterprise, Business, and EU support coming soon.

articles.title: NOW YOU CAN HAVE ALL YOUR APPLE DEVICES IN THE NEW COSMIC ORANGE
articles.summary: Dbrand has released new skins that mimic Apple's Cosmic Orange iPhone 17 Pro color, allowing users to apply the shade to various devices, including MacBooks, iPads, AirPods, Android phones, and Windows laptops. Playfully calling them “knock-offs,” the company offers the skins for prices ranging from about $25 to $65, depending on the device.

articles.title: ADIDAS RELEASES "MOST VISUALLY PLAYFUL BALL EVER" FOR WORLD CUP 2026
articles.summary: Adidas has unveiled Trionda, the official match ball for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, which features red, blue, and green swirls that symbolize the host nations, Canada, the USA, and Mexico. The four-panel design includes embossed national motifs, enhanced surface texture for stability and grip, and a new side-mounted motion sensor chip that provides real-time ball data for VAR decisions.

articles.title: WIRING INTERFACE TO INTENT
articles.summary: LLMs excel at understanding intent and manner, making them powerful tools for creating adaptive interfaces that respond to user requests with appropriate UI components rather than text. This article demonstrates how to build "call-and-response UI" systems where the LLM acts as a design decision-maker, interpreting user intent and returning structured data that tells the interface which component to display, essentially translating natural language into machine-readable interface instructions. By constraining outputs to predefined UI patterns and utilizing prompts as collaborative design specifications, teams can create experiences that adapt to user needs while leveraging LLMs' strengths in understanding context and mitigating their weaknesses in handling facts.

articles.title: FROM PIXELS TO PROMPTS: NAVIGATING THE GENERATIVE AI REVOLUTION IN UX
articles.summary: Generative AI is transforming UX design by automating execution tasks, such as creating prototypes and analyzing feedback, allowing designers to shift from pixel-pushing to strategic roles focused on systems thinking, ethical oversight, and prompt engineering. The technology serves as a collaborative tool across all design phases, but it requires human judgment to ensure that outputs are inclusive, contextually appropriate, and aligned with user needs. UX designers must develop AI literacy and master prompt crafting while maintaining responsibility for critical thinking, as their irreplaceable value lies in asking the right questions rather than merely producing artifacts quickly.

articles.title: THE CREATIVEPRENEUR STARTER PACK FOR 2026: HOW TO MAKE MONEY FROM YOUR TALENT
articles.summary: Creatives today need both artistic skill and business savvy to thrive. The key to success is pricing work confidently, making payments effortless, diversifying sales channels, building credibility through curated portfolios and testimonials, and keeping admin simple to reduce stress, allowing more time for creativity and growth.

articles.title: THE MISSING UI FOR CLAUDE CODE
articles.summary: Run parallel Claudes safely in containers. Jump between their environments to instantly test changes. Get suggestions that catch critical issues as you go.

articles.title: WORLD-CLASS DIGITAL ASSET MANAGEMENT PLATFORM
articles.summary: Pics.io is an AI-powered digital asset management platform that lets you centralize, manage, and distribute all your digital content in one place with

articles.title: NEUMORPHISM GENERATOR
articles.summary: Create soft UI neumorphism effects with visual controls. Perfect for modern, minimal interface designs.

articles.title: INTENT-BASED UI IS REPLACING NAVIGATION—ARE WE DESIGNING OURSELVES OUT OF THE INTERFACE?
articles.summary: Traditional navigation is dying as interfaces shift toward intent-based design, where systems anticipate user needs rather than requiring explicit menu choices—seen in platforms like Spotify, TikTok, and ChatGPT. While this approach excels at routine tasks, it risks stripping users of cognitive maps and autonomy, creating disorienting "black boxes" that trap people in algorithmic bubbles and fail when predictions are wrong. The solution lies in predictive systems that augment, rather than replace, traditional navigation, offering intelligent suggestions while maintaining a transparent structure and user control.

articles.title: BIGGEST TAKEAWAYS FOR UX DESIGNERS FROM FIGMA'S 2025 AI REPORT
articles.summary: Figma's 2025 AI report reveals that AI is fundamentally reshaping design workflows by improving speed and efficiency, though 60% of designers don't yet see quality improvements. The research shows that design skills are becoming increasingly valuable for AI products, with most AI builders considering design at least as critical as for traditional products. However, the unpredictability of AI introduces new complexities in prototyping, user testing, and determining transparency levels. Designers currently use AI primarily for research tasks, such as desk research (38%) and data analysis (40%), rather than for creative work.

articles.title: "OUR BOTTLE HAS ALWAYS BEEN A CANVAS”: HOW ABSOLUT DISTILLED KEITH HARING'S ART
articles.summary: Absolut Vodka has launched a new artist collaboration celebrating Pop Art legend Keith Haring, transforming London's Charing Cross station into “Haring Cross” — an immersive public art gallery featuring Haring's original Absolut works. The Playful Underground campaign continues both Haring's and Absolut's mission to make art accessible to everyone, blending brand heritage, authenticity, and the spirit of Pop Art with an emphasis on joy and inclusivity.

articles.title: HOW TO HANDLE ACCESSIBILITY REPORTING UNDER THE EUROPEAN ACCESSIBILITY ACT (EAA)
articles.summary: Companies covered by the European Accessibility Act must publish an accessibility statement explaining how their services meet the requirements and train their support staff to answer accessibility-related questions.

articles.title: FIVE COMMON MISTAKES MADE BY CREATIVE FREELANCERS JUGGLING INCOMES, BY A TAX EXPERT
articles.summary: Creative freelancers juggling multiple income streams commonly make five critical mistakes when handling taxes, including failing to track every payment, regardless of size, and not understanding HMRC's requirement to declare all income on Self Assessment returns, even with PAYE employment.

articles.title: DESIGNERS REVEAL THE ONE OBJECT THEY CAN'T LIVE WITHOUT
articles.summary: Designers each have a personal object they can't work without—whether it's a chair, notebook, mug, pencil, or book—reflecting how creativity is shaped by comfort, habit, and personal connection as much as by tools or technology.
